 {"id":"br-2bbt.8","title":"[track] Cross-Screen Deep Linking","description":"## Rationale (from webapp parity)

The webapp has clickable links everywhere: agent names link to agent profiles, thread IDs link to thread views, message IDs link to message detail, project slugs link to project overview. The webapp breadcrumb navigation provides back-navigation context.

The TUI should mirror this: navigating between related entities should be fluid and intuitive.

## Implementation

1. DeepLink enum:
   - Agent(project_slug, agent_name) -> Agents screen, agent selected
   - Thread(project_slug, thread_id) -> Threads screen, thread expanded
   - Message(project_slug, message_id) -> Messages screen, message selected + detail open
   - Project(project_slug) -> Projects screen, project selected
   - Reservation(project_slug, reservation_id) -> Reservations screen, reservation selected
   - Search(query) -> Search screen with query pre-filled

2. Navigation stack (breadcrumb-like):
   - Push current screen + scroll position + selection state on navigate
   - Alt+Left pops stack (goes back to previous screen with state restored)
   - Backspace is NOT used (conflicts with text input in search bars, compose panel, etc.)
   - Stack depth limited to 10 entries (circular buffer, oldest dropped)
   - Stack shown in status bar breadcrumb: Dashboard > Messages > Thread #42

3. Deep linking mechanism (TUI-appropriate):
   - Enter on a selected ROW performs the screen's primary action:
     - Messages: expand message detail pane
     - Agents: view agent profile detail
     - Threads: expand thread conversation view
     - Timeline: expand event details
   - Deep linking to REFERENCED entities (e.g., sender name within a message) happens through:
     a. Action Menu (. key): includes "Jump to sender", "Jump to thread" entries (per br-2bbt.5.2)
     b. Command Palette (Ctrl+P): entity search finds agents/threads/messages by name
   - NOTE: TUI tables select whole rows, not individual cells. There is no "click on agent name within a message row" interaction like in the webapp. The Action Menu and Command Palette substitute for this.

4. Visual indicators for linkable contexts:
   - When detail pane shows a message, referenced entities (sender name, thread ID) are rendered in a distinct color (link_color semantic) to hint they're navigable
   - Status bar breadcrumb shows navigation path for context

5. Integration with Command Palette (br-2bbt.1):
   - Palette entity search results create DeepLinks
   - Palette shows recent navigation history as entries

6. Integration with Action Menu (br-2bbt.5):
   - Action menu entries include Jump to sender, Jump to thread, Jump to project
   - These create DeepLinks that push to navigation stack

## Tests
- Unit: DeepLink dispatch for all 6 link types
- Unit: navigation stack push/pop/overflow (>10 entries wraps)
- Unit: state restoration on back (scroll position, selection)
- Snapshot: status bar breadcrumb rendering at different depths

 {"id":"br-2bbt.9","title":"[track] Saved Filter Presets","description":"Save and recall complex filter/sort/group configurations:

1. On any screen with filters, Ctrl+S opens Save Preset dialog (name, optional description)
2. Presets stored in tui_persist state file
3. Ctrl+L opens Load Preset list (shows saved presets for current screen)
4. Delete preset with Del key in Load list
5. Presets are per-screen (Messages presets dont show on Timeline)

TERMINAL NOTE: Ctrl+S traditionally triggers XOFF flow control (freeze output) and
Ctrl+L traditionally clears screen in shell contexts. However, the TUI runs in raw
terminal mode where XOFF/XON is disabled and all key events are captured by the
application. frankentui handles this terminal setup, so Ctrl+S and Ctrl+L are safe
to use as application keybindings within the TUI.

Common preset examples:
- Messages: Unacked high-priority from last 24h
- Timeline: Errors and warnings only
- Reservations: Expiring within 10 minutes
- Explorer: All from specific agent

## Acceptance Criteria
- Save/Load/Delete preset lifecycle works
- Presets persist across TUI restarts
- Presets are screen-specific
- Ctrl+S and Ctrl+L work in raw terminal mode (XOFF disabled)
- Unit tests for preset serialization/deserialization
- Unit tests for save/load/delete lifecycle
- Unit tests for screen-scoped preset isolation","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:52:35.869828743Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T02:15:43.998394849Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["filters","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.9","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:52:35.869828743Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

-{"id":"br-2ca6h","title":"mail send fails with OpenWrite root page 0 on messages insert","description":"## Summary
am mail send fails for registered project/agents with a write-path SQLite error during INSERT INTO messages. This blocks agent introductions and normal coordination messaging.

## Reproduction
1. Ensure project /data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust exists and sender/recipient are registered (e.g. CalmAnchor -> IvoryHill).
2. Run:
   /data/tmp/cargo-target/debug/am mail send --project /data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust --from CalmAnchor --to IvoryHill --subject "[intro] CalmAnchor online" --thread-id onboarding-2026-02-12 --body "CalmAnchor here: onboarding complete, starting bead work now." --importance high --json
3. Observe failure after BEGIN CONCURRENT + message insert attempt.

## Observed Error
SQLite error: Query error: internal error: OpenWrite failed: could not open storage cursor on root page 0

## Notes
- Contact-handshake and reservation short-TTL flows now succeed after explicit-column query fixes (br-59x3r).
- This appears to be a separate write-path failure in message insertion/transaction handling.

## Impact
- Blocks sending intro/progress/completion messages across agents.
- Increases risk of coordination gaps during multi-agent work.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"CalmAnchor","created_at":"2026-02-12T20:52:49.450909591Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T20:53:03.940130491Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["coordination","messaging","sqlite"]}

 {"id":"br-2cdp2","title":"T6.6: E2E test suite for am golden","description":"## Objective

## Work

## Deliverable
An E2E suite proving native golden validation is reliable and migration-ready.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Includes comprehensive unit, integration, and end-to-end coverage for this task's behavior (happy path, failure path, and boundary conditions).
- Produces detailed, machine-readable run artifacts: structured JSON summary, per-step command traces, stdout/stderr capture, timing data, and environment metadata needed for deterministic reproduction.
- Logging is high-signal and explicit: each assertion failure reports scenario ID, input fixture, expected vs actual values, and a recommended next debug step.
- CI execution is deterministic (seed/time controls, explicit timeouts/retries, no hidden external dependencies) and failures are actionable without manual log spelunking.
- Results are wired into standard quality gates so regressions block merges.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:52:51.261116462Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T08:43:27.244788700Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T08:43:27.244770216Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2cdp2","depends_on_id":"br-1pyg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:53:23.355541418Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":263,"issue_id":"br-2cdp2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# T6.6: E2E Test Suite for `am golden`

## What to test

End-to-end validation of the `am golden` subcommand covering capture, verify,
list modes, normalization, and checksum validation.

## Test cases

### test_golden_capture
Run `am golden capture --dir /tmp/test_golden/`:
- All golden files created in /tmp/test_golden/
- checksums.sha256 created with sha256sum format
- Each .golden file has normalized content (no ANSI, no timestamps)

### test_golden_verify_pass
1. Run `am golden capture --dir /tmp/test_golden/`
2. Run `am golden verify --dir /tmp/test_golden/`
- Exit code 0
- Output shows all files passing

### test_golden_verify_fail
1. Run `am golden capture --dir /tmp/test_golden/`
2. Tamper with one .golden file (append extra line)
3. Run `am golden verify --dir /tmp/test_golden/`
- Exit code 1
- Output shows the mismatched file with inline diff
- Checksum mismatch detected

### test_golden_list
Run `am golden list --dir /tmp/test_golden/`:
- Lists all golden file names
- Shows status: present/missing/stale for each
- Includes count summary

### test_golden_filter
Run `am golden capture --dir /tmp/test_golden/ --filter "help"`:
- Only captures files matching "help" pattern
- Other golden files not captured

### test_golden_normalization
Run `am golden capture --dir /tmp/test_golden/` and verify:
- No ANSI escape sequences in any .golden file
- No ISO-8601 timestamps (replaced with <TIMESTAMP>)
- No PIDs (replaced with <PID>)
- No absolute home paths (replaced with <HOME>/...)
- No version numbers (replaced with <VERSION>)

### test_golden_checksum_format
Verify checksums.sha256 format:
- Each line matches: "<64-char-hex>  <filename>"
- Two spaces between hash and filename (sha256sum standard)
- All listed files exist in the golden directory

### test_golden_json_output
Run `am golden verify --dir /tmp/test_golden/ --json`:
- Valid JSON output
- Contains per-file results with pass/fail status
- Summary with total/pass/fail counts

### test_golden_mcp_denial_captures
Run `am golden capture --dir /tmp/test_golden/`:
- Verify MCP denial golden files captured from mcp-agent-mail binary
- Content should be the deterministic denial message
- Exit code 2 from mcp-agent-mail correctly handled

### test_golden_stub_encoder_captures
Run `am golden capture --dir /tmp/test_golden/`:
- Verify stub encoder golden files captured
- Content is deterministic for given inputs

## Implementation notes
- Create as tests/e2e/test_golden.sh
- Use a temp directory for golden files (not the repo's benches/golden/)
- Clean up temp directory after tests
- Verify normalization by checking for absence of ANSI regex pattern
- Use `shasum -a 256` or `sha256sum` to independently verify checksums

Tests template detection, rate limiting, HTTP/direct modes (conditional on seeding), error handling. 11 cases, 17 assertions pass, 2 skipped due to pre-existing register_agent bug.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3qjmi","depends_on_id":"br-erlj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:53:19.164684775Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":261,"issue_id":"br-3qjmi","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# T4.7: E2E Test Suite for `am check-inbox`

## What to test

End-to-end validation of `am check-inbox` covering rate limiting, MCP client,
direct DB query, urgency detection, template detection, and failure silence.

## Test cases

### test_check_inbox_no_mail
Start a server with empty inbox, run `am check-inbox`:
- Should produce NO output (silent when empty)
- Exit code 0

### test_check_inbox_with_mail
Start a server, send a message to test agent, run `am check-inbox`:
- Should output "📬 1 unread messages"
- Exit code 0

### test_check_inbox_urgent_mail
Send a message with importance:"urgent", run `am check-inbox`:
- Should output "⚠️ 1 urgent" warning line
- Should also show total unread count

### test_check_inbox_rate_limit
Run `am check-inbox` twice within 120 seconds:
- First call: produces output (or silence if no mail)
- Second call: silent exit (rate limited)
- After 120s (or with --force): runs again

### test_check_inbox_custom_interval
Run `am check-inbox --interval 5` twice:
- First call: runs
- Wait 2 seconds, second call: rate limited (silent)
- Wait 4 more seconds, third call: runs again

### test_check_inbox_template_detection
Set AGENT_MAIL_PROJECT="YOUR_PROJECT", run `am check-inbox`:
- Silent exit, no error output
- Same for AGENT_MAIL_AGENT="PLACEHOLDER_AGENT"
- Same for AGENT_MAIL_AGENT="<your-agent-name>"

### test_check_inbox_connection_failure
Run `am check-inbox` with invalid URL (port 1):
- Silent exit (exit code 0, no error output)
- Must NOT interrupt agent work

### test_check_inbox_direct_mode
Run `am check-inbox --direct` with a seeded DB:
- Should query SQLite directly (no HTTP)
- Same output format as HTTP mode
- Faster execution

### test_check_inbox_json_output
Run `am check-inbox --json` with mail present:
- Valid JSON output with unread_count, urgent_count, messages array

### test_check_inbox_env_vars
Verify AGENT_MAIL_* environment variables work:
- AGENT_MAIL_PROJECT sets project
- AGENT_MAIL_AGENT sets agent
- AGENT_MAIL_URL sets server URL
- AGENT_MAIL_TOKEN sets bearer token
- AGENT_MAIL_INTERVAL sets rate limit interval

## Implementation notes
- Create as tests/e2e/test_check_inbox.sh
- Need to start an am server for HTTP mode tests
- Need seeded DB for direct mode tests
- Test rate limiting by manipulating lockfile mtime
- All error scenarios must verify SILENT exit (no stderr output)
","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:53:01Z"},{"id":279,"issue_id":"br-3qjmi","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## SECOND-PASS CORRECTION: T4.7 E2E test for am check-inbox

Fix test cases based on deep audit:

1. test_check_inbox_rate_limit: The lockfile stores Unix SECONDS as text content
   (via `echo $NOW > file`), NOT mtime. Verify by reading lockfile contents.
   Also the interval is 120 seconds by default — don't wait that long in tests.
   Use --interval 2 for fast testing.

2. Add test_check_inbox_lockfile_format:
   Run check-inbox, verify lockfile exists at /tmp/mcp-mail-check-{AGENT}
   Verify contents is a Unix timestamp (integer seconds)
   Verify filename sanitization: "my-agent.v2" → "my_agent_v2"

3. test_check_inbox_connection_failure: The curl has --max-time 3.
   Use port 1 for instant connection refusal, verify silent exit within 4 seconds.

4. test_check_inbox_with_mail: The exact output format must include:
   - Blank line before
   - "📬 === INBOX REMINDER ==="
   - Indented message text (3 spaces)
   - "=========================" (25 equals)
   - Blank line after

5. Add test_check_inbox_url_default:
   Verify default URL is http://127.0.0.1:8765/api/ (note: /api/ not /mcp/)

6. test_check_inbox_json_output: This is a NEW feature (bash has no --json).
   Mark as enhancement test.

7. test_check_inbox_direct_mode: This is also a NEW feature.
   Mark as enhancement test.

 {"id":"br-3sexn","title":"R2.3: Implement am robot timeline — events since last check with --since/--kind/--source filters","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:16:58.197083921Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T05:48:06.230490970Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T05:48:06.230427801Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3sexn","depends_on_id":"br-1nmyh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:20:46.997509638Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":305,"issue_id":"br-3sexn","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# R2.3: `am robot timeline`

## What
Chronological event stream since a given timestamp or "last check". Tracks a "last seen" watermark so agents can call it repeatedly and only see new events.

## Watermark Mechanism
- Store last-check timestamp in a lockfile at `/tmp/mcp-mail-robot-timeline-{agent_sanitized}`
- Format: file contains Unix timestamp (seconds) as text content
- On each invocation:
  1. Read watermark file → `since_ts`
  2. Query events since `since_ts`
  3. Update watermark to current time
  4. If `--since` flag provided, ignore watermark and use that instead

## Event Sources
Events come from the messages table + timeline data:
1. **message** — new messages received (created_at > since_ts)
2. **ack** — messages acknowledged (ack_ts > since_ts, ack_ts IS NOT NULL)
3. **read** — messages read (read_at > since_ts, read_at IS NOT NULL)
4. **reservation** — file reservations created/expired
5. **agent** — new agents registered

## Flags
- `--since <duration|timestamp>` — Override watermark. Accepts:
  - Duration: `5m`, `1h`, `24h`, `7d`
  - ISO-8601: `2026-02-11T10:00:00Z`
  - Default: from watermark file (or 1h if no watermark)
- `--kind <type>` — Filter to event kind: message, ack, read, reservation, agent
- `--source <src>` — Filter by source (db, git — reserved for future git event tracking)

## Output Format
```
_meta.since: 2026-02-11T10:00:00Z
_meta.until: 2026-02-11T10:30:00Z
_meta.total_events: 8

events[8]{seq,timestamp,kind,summary}:
  1,10:02,message,BlueLake → RedFox: [FEAT-123] Starting auth
  2,10:05,reservation,BlueLake reserved src/auth/** (exclusive, 1h)
  3,10:10,ack,RedFox ack'd message #142
  4,10:15,message,GreenCastle → all: [DB-001] Schema change proposal
  5,10:18,read,BlueLake read message #156
  6,10:20,reservation,RedFox released src/middleware/**
  7,10:25,message,SilverWolf → BlueLake: [PLAN-001] Sprint plan
  8,10:28,agent,CopperIsland registered (codex-cli, gpt-5.2)
```

## Duration Parsing
- `5m` → 5 * 60 seconds ago
- `1h` → 3600 seconds ago
- `24h` → 86400 seconds ago
- `7d` → 7 * 86400 seconds ago
- ISO-8601 → parse directly

## Acceptance Criteria
- Watermark file created/updated on each invocation
- Events sorted chronologically (oldest first)
- --kind filter works correctly
- Duration parsing handles m/h/d suffixes
- Without --since and no watermark file, defaults to 1h

Added PortStatus enum, check_port_status() function, and 8 new tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":217,"issue_id":"br-7ri2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# T5.1: Implement Cross-Platform Port-in-Use Detection

## What to build
Detect whether a port is already in use, and if so, whether it's an Agent Mail
server (reuse) or another process (error). Replaces the lsof-based detection
in scripts/am (lines 151-168).

## Current bash behavior
```bash
listener_pid=$(lsof -tiTCP:${port} -sTCP:LISTEN 2>/dev/null | head -n1)
listener_cmd=$(ps -p "$listener_pid" -o args= 2>/dev/null)
if [[ "$listener_cmd" == *"mcp_agent_mail"* ]]; then
    echo "reusing existing server"
    exit 0
fi
```

## Problems
- lsof is Linux-specific (different syntax on macOS, unavailable on Windows)
- ps -o args= format varies across platforms
- Race condition: process could exit between lsof and ps

## Rust implementation
```rust
enum PortStatus {
    Free,
    AgentMailServer { pid: u32 },
    OtherProcess { pid: Option<u32>, description: String },
}

fn check_port(host: &str, port: u16) -> PortStatus {
    // Strategy 1: Try to bind
    match TcpListener::bind((host, port)) {
        Ok(_listener) => {
            // Port is free (listener dropped immediately)
            return PortStatus::Free;
        }
        Err(e) if e.kind() == ErrorKind::AddrInUse => {
            // Port is in use, try to identify the process
        }
        Err(e) => {
            // Other error (permission denied, etc.)
        }
    }

    // Strategy 2: Try to connect and check if it's Agent Mail
    // Send a health check request to the expected path
    // If we get an Agent Mail response, it's our server
    match try_health_check(host, port) {
        Ok(true) => PortStatus::AgentMailServer { pid: 0 },
        _ => PortStatus::OtherProcess { ... },
    }
}
```

## Implementation notes
- Primary detection via TcpListener::bind() (portable, no external tools)
- Server identification via HTTP health check to /mcp/health or /api/health
  (the server already has health endpoints: COMPAT_HEALTH_PATHS in server crate)
- No need for PID detection — just knowing it's Agent Mail is sufficient for reuse
- This approach works on all platforms without platform-specific code

## Location
crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/startup_checks.rs (or new port_check.rs module)
","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:34:00Z"},{"id":273,"issue_id":"br-7ri2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## SECOND-PASS CORRECTION: Track 5 Real Gaps

### T5.2 — .env File Search is a REAL GAP
The Rust config.rs loads .env from the CURRENT WORKING DIRECTORY only.
The bash scripts/am searches these specific paths:
  1. ~/.mcp_agent_mail/.env (preferred)
  2. ~/mcp_agent_mail/.env (legacy, no dot prefix)

This is a REAL gap — when agents run from their project directory (e.g., /data/projects/foo),
the CWD .env won't have the Agent Mail token. The bash wrapper handles this by
explicitly searching the home-directory-based paths.

T5.2 should add the home-directory .env search to Config::from_env() or
to the serve command startup, using the same precedence as bash:
  1. Environment variable (already works)
  2. ~/.mcp_agent_mail/.env
  3. ~/mcp_agent_mail/.env
  4. CWD .env (already works)

### T5.1 — Port Detection Limitations
The bash uses lsof to get the PID of the listener. TcpListener::bind() only tells
you if the port is available, NOT what process holds it.

For full parity, Rust needs to either:
a) Shell out to `lsof -tiTCP:{port} -sTCP:LISTEN` (platform-specific but matches bash)
b) Read /proc/net/tcp on Linux (more robust, no external dep)
c) Just try to bind and report the error (simpler but loses PID info)

Option (a) is recommended for parity. The PID is needed to check if the
existing process is mcp_agent_mail / mcp-agent-mail (vs a foreign process).

### T5.4 — --env-file flag
The bash supports --env-file <path> to override the default .env search.
Check if the Rust main.rs already has this flag. If not, add it.

## What it replaces
scripts/ci.sh (287 lines of bash)

## Current behavior
The bash script runs 13 quality/performance/security/docs gates sequentially:
- cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy, cargo build, cargo test
- mode_matrix_harness, semantic_conformance, perf_security_regressions, help_snapshots
- Release docs presence check
- E2E suites (dual-mode, mode matrix, security/privacy, TUI accessibility)

It tracks pass/fail/skip per gate, times each gate with `date +%s` (1-second granularity),
and generates a structured JSON gate report using a 100-line jq pipeline. Supports
--quick (skip E2E) and --report <path> flags.

## External dependencies eliminated
- **jq**: The entire JSON gate report (lines 103-276) depends on jq. Rust's serde_json
  handles this natively with proper typing.

## Key improvements
1. **Parallel gate execution**: Bash runs all gates sequentially. Independent gates
   (fmt + clippy + docs check) can run simultaneously, cutting total CI time by 30-40%.
2. **Sub-millisecond timing**: date +%s gives 1-second granularity. Rust Instant gives
   nanosecond precision — critical for benchmarking fast gates.
3. **Structured error capture**: When a gate fails, bash just says "FAIL". Rust can
   capture stderr, parse compiler/test output, and produce actionable failure details.
4. **Cross-platform**: No bash-specific constructs, works on Windows with cargo.
5. **Typed gate report**: The JSON report schema (am_ci_gate_report.v1) becomes a proper
   Rust struct with serde, eliminating the 100-line jq pipeline.

## CLI interface
```
am ci [--quick] [--report <path>] [--parallel] [--json]
```

## Implementation location
crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/ci.rs (new module)
Wire into Cli enum in crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs

## Files to read for context
- scripts/ci.sh (the script being replaced)
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs (CLI structure to extend)
- tests/artifacts/ci/gate_report.json (example output format)

## What to build
Save, load, and compare benchmark baselines for regression detection. Replaces
the BENCH_BASELINE_FILE logic in the Python aggregation script.

## Key behaviors
1. **Save baseline**: Write current benchmark results to a JSON file (--save-baseline)
2. **Load baseline**: Read a previously saved baseline file
3. **Compare**: For each benchmark, compute delta_p95_ms = current.p95 - baseline.p95
4. **Regression detection**: Flag if delta_p95_ms > threshold (default: 10% of baseline)

## Rust implementation
```rust
/// Baseline file format: map of benchmark name → p95_ms value
type BaselineData = BTreeMap<String, f64>;

fn save_baseline(results: &BTreeMap<String, BenchResult>, path: &Path) -> Result<(), Error>

fn load_baseline(path: &Path) -> Result<BaselineData, Error>

fn compare_baseline(
    results: &BTreeMap<String, BenchResult>,
    baseline: &BaselineData,
    threshold_pct: f64,   // e.g. 0.10 for 10%
) -> Vec<BaselineComparison>

struct BaselineComparison {
    name: String,
    current_p95_ms: f64,
    baseline_p95_ms: f64,
    delta_p95_ms: f64,
    delta_pct: f64,
    regression: bool,
}
```

## Implementation notes
- Baseline file is simple JSON: `{"help": 2.5, "mail_inbox": 15.3, ...}`
- This matches the format expected by the Python script (BENCH_BASELINE_FILE)
- Benchmarks not present in the baseline get baseline_p95_ms=null, regression=false
- Regression threshold is configurable but defaults to 10%
- Print regression warnings in human-readable output (highlighted in red if terminal)

## Location
crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/bench.rs (baseline functions)

  - If identifiers empty → no deletions.
  - Match identifiers case‑insensitive by slug or human_key; error on unknown.

- Unit tests for identifier matching + error cases.

- Save before/after row counts under `tests/artifacts/share/snapshot/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Snapshot creation refuses overwrite and produces byte‑identical DB backup.
2. Project scoping deletes only disallowed project data in the specified order.
3. ## What to build
Add a `Ci` variant to the CLI enum in lib.rs and handle it by orchestrating the
gate runner and report generator.

## CLI interface
```
am ci [--quick] [--report <path>] [--json]
```

Flags:
- --quick: Skip gates marked skip_in_quick (E2E suites). Always produces decision=no-go.
- --report <path>: Write gate report to this path (default: tests/artifacts/ci/gate_report.json)
- --json: Machine-readable JSON output to stdout (summary only, full report goes to --report)

## Behavior
1. Detect project root (git rev-parse or CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR)
2. Set up environment variables (CARGO_TARGET_DIR, DATABASE_URL, etc.)
3. Create output directories (mkdir -p for report path)
4. Run all gates via run_gates()
5. Generate report via generate_report()
6. Print summary to stderr:
   ```
   ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
     CI Summary: Pass=N  Fail=N  Skip=N
   ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
     Gate report: <path>
     RESULT: PASSED | FAILED
   ```
7. Exit code: 0 if decision=go, 1 if decision=no-go

## Implementation notes
- Add `Ci(CiArgs)` to the Cli enum in crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs
- CiArgs struct with clap derives for --quick, --report, --json
- The 13 default gates should be hardcoded as a constant (matching ci.sh lines 143-171)
- Per-gate output should be streamed to stderr in real-time (not just at the end),
  so the user sees progress. Use Command::spawn() + pipe rather than Command::output()
  for real-time streaming.

## Location
crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs (Cli enum addition)
crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/ci.rs (run_ci_command function)

The bash ci.sh supports these flags:
  --quick        Skip long-running E2E gates
  --report <path>  Write gate report JSON to specified path
                   (default: tests/artifacts/ci/gate_report.json)
  -h, --help     Show help

The Rust `am ci` should support:
  --quick          Skip gates marked skip_in_quick=true
  --report <path>  Specify JSON report output path
  --json           Print report JSON to stdout (NEW, not in bash)
  --gate <name>    Run only a specific gate (NEW, not in bash)
  --parallel       Run independent gates concurrently (NEW, T1.5)
  -h, --help       Show help

Environment variable:
  CI_GATE_REPORT_PATH — overrides default report path
  (bash: REPORT_PATH="${CI_GATE_REPORT_PATH:-tests/artifacts/ci/gate_report.json}")

 {"id":"br-dcnr4","title":"R3.3: Implement am robot message <id> — single message with thread position, adjacent messages, sender info","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:17:10.023690650Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T05:13:12.087661783Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T05:13:12.087597984Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-dcnr4","depends_on_id":"br-1nmyh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:20:51.275518484Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":310,"issue_id":"br-dcnr4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# R3.3: `am robot message <id>`

## What
Single message with full context: body, thread position, adjacent messages, sender info.
Useful when an agent has a message ID from an alert or inbox and wants to understand context.

## Data Collection
1. Fetch the target message by ID
2. Fetch sender info (program, model) from agents table
3. Fetch thread context: total messages in thread, position of this message
4. Fetch previous message summary (same thread, created_at < this message, LIMIT 1 DESC)
5. Fetch next message summary (same thread, created_at > this message, LIMIT 1 ASC)
6. Fetch attachment list for this message

## Output Format (Markdown default)
```markdown
## Message #201 | Thread: FEAT-123 (3 of 8)

**From**: BlueLake (claude-code, opus-4.6)
**To**: RedFox, GreenCastle
**Subject**: [FEAT-123] JWT implementation plan
**Importance**: high | **Ack**: required (pending)
**Sent**: 2026-02-11T08:30:00Z (2h ago)

---

I'm taking on the authentication feature. Planning to use JWT with JWKS rotation.

Will need to modify:
- src/auth/**
- src/middleware/**
- tests/auth/**

Please review the approach and ack.

---

**Attachments**: 1
- api_spec.json (8KB, application/json)

**← Previous**: #200 RedFox: "I'll handle the middleware setup"
**→ Next**: #202 RedFox: "Sounds good, releasing middleware reservations"
```

## Output (TOON)
```
message.id: 201
message.thread: FEAT-123
message.position: 3 of 8
message.from: BlueLake
message.from_program: claude-code
message.from_model: opus-4.6
message.to: RedFox,GreenCastle
message.subject: [FEAT-123] JWT implementation plan
message.importance: high
message.ack_status: required (pending)
message.age: 2h
message.body: |
  I'm taking on the authentication feature...

attachments[1]{name,size,type}:
  api_spec.json,8KB,application/json

context.previous: #200 RedFox: I'll handle the middleware setup
context.next: #202 RedFox: Sounds good, releasing middleware reservations
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Full message body always included
